title: automaton-model-sheet-boardanchor-2k
prompt:
A hero-prop / character model sheet for animation production, on a clean plain white studio background.

SUBJECT — the reference image shows the canonical GOLD AUTOMATON design. Reproduce THIS exact design as a multi-angle turntable. Design spec (match exactly):
- Head: a cylindrical, helmet-like skullcap, separated from the face by a distinct HORIZONTAL SEAM running across the forehead. Rigid blocky side panels frame the face.
- Face: a stylized Greco-Roman mask — hollow, pupil-less almond eyes; a straight, prominent nose bridge; a flat unsmiling horizontal-line mouth; a strong squared jawline.
- Surface: brass / burnished gold, semi-polished brushed metal with painterly facets (NOT mirror-chrome, NOT flat dead matte). Warm yellow highlights, cool blue-teal reflected shadow.
- Construction: prominent CIRCULAR BALL-AND-SOCKET joints at the shoulders; a clear seam where the neck meets the collar.
- Body: heroic humanoid proportions — broad blocky shoulders, thick cylindrical neck, tapered torso.
This is the canonical, mass-produced design — every figure identical. Do NOT add a crown, hat, crest, hair, or glowing eyes; do NOT smooth the face into a featureless blank mannequin.

LAYOUT — a multi-angle turntable spread, evenly and identically lit across all views:
- full-body FRONT view
- full-body THREE-QUARTER FRONT view
- full-body BACK view
plus two detail callout panels: a head/face close-up showing the forehead seam and almond eye-sockets, and a shoulder close-up showing the ball-and-socket joint.

STYLE — painterly cinematic concept-art rendering matching the reference's medium (brushed gold, painterly facets, warm-gold-on-cool palette). NOT photoreal, NOT a flat line drawing.

Clean white background, no environment, no other figures, only faint angle markers. The reference shows the figure only from the chest up — invent the lower body, legs and feet consistently in the same design language. 16:9 aspect ratio.
